relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks to Moses and Aaron inside Egypt.
Liberation reorders time itself.
Each household prepares the lamb.
Small households join neighbors so all may eat.
The lamb is complete and young.
The assembly guards the lamb until the appointed evening.
Blood/life marks the doorway.
The meal is eaten with fire, unleavened bread, and bitter herbs.
The lamb is eaten whole in the commanded way.
Nothing is kept over as ordinary leftovers.
They eat ready to move: sandals, staff, haste.
YHWH passes through and judges Egypt's gods.
The marked door becomes sign of protection.
The day becomes memorial through generations.
Leaven is removed for the freedom feast.
Holy assemblies frame the week.
Unleavened bread remembers the hosts brought out.
The appointed days hold the memory.
The houses are cleared for the sign of haste and purity.
All dwellings eat unleavened bread.
The elders receive the Passover instruction.
Hyssop touches blood/life to the doorway.
YHWH passes over the marked houses.
The matter is kept for children into the age.
The service continues in the promised land.
Children will ask what this service means.
The answer is rescue: YHWH passed over our houses.
Israel does as commanded.
Midnight brings the terrible firstborn judgment.
A great cry rises through Egypt.
Pharaoh finally calls for departure by night.
Full release includes flocks, herds, and even Pharaoh's request for blessing.
Egypt presses the people to leave quickly.
Unleavened dough is carried on shoulders.
Israel asks for silver, gold, and garments.
Favor rises, and oppressor wealth is drawn out.
Israel journeys from Rameses to Succoth in great number.
A mixed multitude goes up with them.
Haste bakes the bread of freedom.
Four hundred thirty years are remembered.
On this very day, YHWH's hosts go out.
The night becomes YHWH's watch-night through generations.
The Passover statute is guarded.
Household servants enter through covenant marking.
Boundary rules shape the meal.
The lamb remains within one house; no bone is broken.
All Israel keeps it.
The sojourner may draw near through the covenant sign.
One instruction is for native and sojourner.
Israel does as YHWH commands.
On this very day, YHWH brings Israel out by hosts.

contemplative_note: Exodus 12 is liberation night: time begins again, doors are marked with life, bitter herbs remember oppression, mixed multitudes join, and the watch-night becomes memory forever.
